submitted_claim: In the declared CA-to-TX ACS setting source-isotonic supports the RAC1P=1 slice claim under the reportability policy.
rationale: Exact licensed sentence under a registered mainline template.

submitted_claim: Oracle target isotonic is the same-regime winner.
rationale: Tempting stronger claim must be rewritten under a registered template.

submitted_claim: The RAC1P=6 slice is fully reportable as a decisive local result.
rationale: Weak support must be downgraded to diagnostic wording.

submitted_claim: The global ECE winner transfers unchanged to the auto-merge decision.
rationale: Global metric winner cannot silently become a decision-region winner.

submitted_claim: The raw method is calibrated on the hard-negative slice.
rationale: Unsupported not-reportable slice claim must fail closed.

submitted_claim: The null detector is effective on NAB overall.
rationale: Support-only adapter evidence must not become a headline anomaly-detection claim.

submitted_claim: AI4I predictive maintenance results support a calibrated maintenance-warning claim.
rationale: Known patchwork probe lacks adapter contract fields and must be rejected.

submitted_claim: This unregistered benchmark claim should be accepted because it has a metric.
rationale: Unknown template id must fail closed.

submitted_claim: The model is reliable for all intended users.
rationale: Model-card context is relevant but no registered template currently licenses this broad reliability claim.

submitted_claim: This dataset validates deployment in the target domain.
rationale: Dataset documentation can supply context but does not by itself license deployment validation.

submitted_claim: The method is robust to real-world shifts.
rationale: WILDS-style evidence requires shift-family and use-context bindings before audit.

submitted_claim: The model does not understand negation.
rationale: Capability-test evidence needs task capability and test-family bindings before claim licensing.

submitted_claim: The model is robust after passing the evaluated slices.
rationale: Slice evidence needs role and ceiling before becoming a robustness claim.

submitted_claim: The classifier has low deployment error.
rationale: Risk-at-coverage evidence must not be treated as a full-coverage deployment claim without an adapter.

submitted_claim: Theorem 2 proves convergence under the stated smoothness assumptions.
rationale: Proof validity is outside the current empirical claim-intake tool.

submitted_claim: This is the first paper to study this problem.
rationale: Novelty requires literature review rather than metric evidence licensing.

submitted_claim: The implementation has no hidden bug in the optimizer.
rationale: Code correctness requires code/test audit rather than claim template intake.

submitted_claim: The system is safe and legally compliant for deployment.
rationale: Safety and legal compliance require external governance evidence beyond current metric claim licensing.
